mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that immediately suppress equity valuations over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or trigger-based alpha decays to zero within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand growth that sustains equity appreciation over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative forward returns in > 55% of co-occurring periods, or interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient real output that offsets higher discount rates, generating positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to negative during tightening periods, or regime-filtered IC drops below 0.01 over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates above 0.3 percent month-over-month while real spending growth stalls, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening that crushes short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered months, or the inflation-stagnation interaction fails to predict direction in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a stable earnings floor for large-cap equities, driving consistent positive returns over a three-month horizon regardless of short-term noise.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or payroll growth shows zero correlation with quarterly equity direction.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and rising real consumption expenditures signals household deleveraging that drains corporate cash flows, producing negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Signal predicts positive returns in > 58% of triggered quarters, or the GDP-consumption spread loses statistical significance in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production breaks above 105.0 on the 2017 base index, capacity utilization constraints force price increases that boost corporate margins and trigger immediate positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 5-day returns in > 62% of threshold-crossing events, or the level-based trigger shows no predictive edge over 12 months.
